children.heading: Awards and settlements
children.label: a Awards and settlements
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (c), only funds which are appropriated to an account of the Office in the Treasury of the United States for the payment of awards and settlements may be used for the payment of awards and settlements under this chapter. There are appropriated for such account such sums as may be necessary to pay such awards and settlements. Funds in the account are not available for awards and settlements involving the Government Accountability Office or the Government Publishing Office.

children.heading: Compliance
children.label: b Compliance
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (c), there are authorized to be appropriated such sums as may be necessary for administrative, personnel, and similar expenses of employing offices which are needed to comply with this chapter.

children.heading: OSHA, accommodation, and access requirements
children.label: c OSHA, accommodation, and access requirements
children.text: Funds to correct violations of section 1311(a)(3), 1331, or 1341 of this title may be paid only from funds appropriated to the employing office or entity responsible for correcting such violations. There are authorized to be appropriated such sums as may be necessary for such funds.

children.heading: Reimbursement by Members of Congress of amounts paid as settlements and awards
children.label: d Reimbursement by Members of Congress of amounts paid as settlements and awards

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraphs (B) and (D), if a payment is made from the account described in subsection (a) for an award or settlement in connection with a claim alleging a violation described in subparagraph (C) committed personally by an individual who, at the time of committing the violation, was a Member of the House of Representatives (including a Delegate or Resident Commissioner to the Congress) or a Senator, the individual shall reimburse the account for the amount of the award or settlement for the claim involved.

children.heading: Conditions
children.label: B Conditions
children.text: In the case of an award made pursuant to a decision of a hearing officer under , or a court in a civil action, subparagraph (A) shall apply only if the hearing officer or court makes a separate finding that a violation described in subparagraph (C) occurred which was committed personally by an individual who, at the time of committing the violation, was a Member of the House of Representatives (including a Delegate or Resident Commissioner to the Congress) or a Senator, and such individual shall reimburse the account for the amount of compensatory damages included in the award as would be available if awarded under irrespective of the size of the employing office. In the case of a settlement for a claim described in , subparagraph (A) shall apply only if the conditions specified in for requesting reimbursement are met.section 1405 of this titlesection 1981a(b)(3) of title 42section 1416(d)(3) of this titlesection 1416(d)(3) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: harassment that is unlawful under section 1311(a) or 1316(a) of this title; or

children.label: ii
children.text: intimidation, reprisal, or discrimination that is unlawful under and is taken against a covered employee because of a claim alleging a violation described in clause (i).section 1317 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: described in subparagraph (A), subject to subparagraph (B); and

children.label: ii
children.text: included in the portion of the award or settlement attributable to a claim requiring reimbursement.

children.heading: Establishment of timetable and procedures by committees
children.label: A Establishment of timetable and procedures by committees
children.text: For purposes of carrying out subparagraph (B), the applicable Committee shall establish a timetable and procedures for the withholding of amounts from the compensation of an individual who is a Member of the House of Representatives or a Senator.

children.heading: Deadline
children.label: B Deadline
children.text: The payroll administrator shall withhold from an individual’s compensation and transfer to the account described in subsection (a) (after making any deposit required under ) such amounts as may be necessary to reimburse the account described in subsection (a) for the reimbursable portion of the award or settlement described in paragraph (1) if the individual has not reimbursed the account as required under paragraph (1) prior to the expiration of the 90-day period which begins on the date a payment is made from the account for such an award or settlement.section 8432(f) of title 5

children.label: i
children.text: the Committee on House Administration of the House of Representatives, in the case of an individual who, at the time of the withholding, is a Member of the House; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the Committee on Rules and Administration of the Senate, in the case of an individual who, at the time of the withholding, is a Senator.

children.heading: Use of amounts in Thrift Savings Fund as source of reimbursement
children.label: 3 Use of amounts in Thrift Savings Fund as source of reimbursement

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If, by the expiration of the 180-day period that begins on the date a payment is made from the account described in subsection (a) for an award or settlement described in paragraph (1), an individual who is subject to a reimbursement requirement of this subsection has not reimbursed the account for the entire reimbursable portion as required under paragraph (1), withholding and transfers of amounts shall continue under paragraph (2) if the individual remains employed in the same position, and the Executive Director of the Federal Retirement Thrift Investment Board shall make a transfer described in subparagraph (B).

children.label: i
children.text: any amount the individual has reimbursed, taking into account any amounts withheld under paragraph (2); and

children.label: ii
children.text: if the individual remains employed in the same position, any amount that the individual is scheduled to reimburse, taking into account any amounts to be withheld under the individual’s timetable under paragraph (2).

children.heading: Initiation of transfer
children.label: C Initiation of transfer
children.text: Notwithstanding , the Executive Director described in subparagraph (A) shall make the transfer under subparagraph (A) upon receipt of a written request to the Executive Director from the Secretary of the Treasury, in the form and manner required by the Executive Director.section 8435 of title 5

children.heading: Coordination between payroll administrator and the Executive Director
children.label: D Coordination between payroll administrator and the Executive Director
children.text: The payroll administrator and the Executive Director described in subparagraph (A) shall carry out this paragraph in a manner that ensures the coordination of the withholding and transferring of amounts under this paragraph, in accordance with regulations promulgated by the Board under and such Executive Director.section 1383 of this title

children.heading: Administrative wage garnishment or other collection of wages from a subsequent position
children.label: 4 Administrative wage garnishment or other collection of wages from a subsequent position

children.heading: Individual subject to garnishment or other collection
children.label: A Individual subject to garnishment or other collection
i
children.text: has not reimbursed the account for the entire reimbursable portion as required under paragraph (1), through withholdings or transfers under paragraphs (2) and (3);

children.label: ii
children.text: is not serving in a position as a Member of the House of Representatives or a Senator; and

children.label: iii
children.text: is employed in a subsequent non-Federal position.

children.heading: Garnishment or other collection of wages
children.label: B Garnishment or other collection of wages
children.text: On the expiration of that 270-day period, the amount of the reimbursable portion of an award or settlement described in paragraph (1) (reduced by any amount the individual has reimbursed, taking into account any amounts withheld or transferred under paragraph (2) or (3)) shall be treated as a claim of the United States and transferred to the Secretary of the Treasury for collection. Upon that transfer, the Secretary of the Treasury shall collect the claim, in accordance with , including by administrative wage garnishment of the wages of the individual described in subparagraph (A) from the position described in subparagraph (A)(iii). The Secretary of the Treasury shall transfer the collected amount to the account described in subsection (a).section 3711 of title 31

children.heading: Notification to Office of Personnel Management and Secretary of the Treasury
children.label: 5 Notification to Office of Personnel Management and Secretary of the Treasury

children.heading: Individual subject to annuity or social security withholding
children.label: A Individual subject to annuity or social security withholding
i
children.text: has not served in a position as a Member of the House of Representatives or a Senator during the preceding 90 days; and

children.label: ii
children.text: is not employed in a subsequent non-Federal position.

children.heading: Annuity or social security withholding
children.label: B Annuity or social security withholding
i
children.text: shall notify the Director of the Office of Personnel Management, who shall take such actions as the Director considers appropriate to withhold from any annuity payable to the individual under chapter 83 or chapter 84 of title 5 and transfer to the account described in subsection (a), such amounts as may be necessary to reimburse the account for the remainder of the reimbursable portion of an award or settlement described in paragraph (1); and

children.label: ii
children.text: shall (if necessary), notwithstanding section 207 of the Social Security Act (), take such actions as the Secretary of the Treasury considers appropriate to withhold from any payment to the individual under title II of the Social Security Act ( et seq.) and transfer to the account described in subsection (a), such amounts as may be necessary to reimburse the account for the remainder of the reimbursable portion of an award or settlement described in paragraph (1).42 U.S.C. 40742 U.S.C. 401

children.heading: Coordination between OPM and Treasury
children.label: 6 Coordination between OPM and Treasury
children.text: The Director of the Office of Personnel Management and the Secretary of the Treasury shall carry out paragraph (5) in a manner that ensures the coordination of the withholding and transferring of amounts under such paragraph, in accordance with regulations promulgated by the Director and the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: the Committees on House Administration and Ethics of the House of Representatives, in the case of an individual who, at the time of committing the act involved, was a Member of the House of Representatives (including a Delegate or Resident Commissioner to the Congress); and

children.label: B
children.text: the Select Committee on Ethics of the Senate, in the case of an individual who, at the time of committing the act involved, was a Senator.

children.heading: Right to intervene
children.label: 8 Right to intervene
children.text: An individual who is subject to a reimbursement requirement of this subsection shall have the unconditional right to intervene in any mediation, hearing, or civil action under this subchapter to protect the interests of the individual in the determination of whether an award or settlement described in paragraph (1) should be made, and the amount of any such award or settlement, except that nothing in this paragraph may be construed to require the covered employee who filed the claim to be deposed by counsel for the individual in a deposition that is separate from any other deposition taken from the employee in connection with the hearing or civil action.

children.heading: Non-Federal position
children.label: A Non-Federal position
children.text: The term “non-Federal position” means a position other than the position of an employee, as defined in .section 2105(a) of title 5

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of an individual who is a Member of the House of Representatives, the Chief Administrative Officer of the House of Representatives, or an employee of the Office of the Chief Administrative Officer who is designated by the Chief Administrative Officer to carry out this subsection; or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of an individual who is a Senator, the Secretary of the Senate, or an employee of the Office of the Secretary of the Senate who is designated by the Secretary to carry out this subsection.

children.heading: Notification of payments made from account
children.label: 1 Notification of payments made from account
children.text: As soon as practicable after the Executive Director is made aware that a payment of an award or settlement under this chapter has been made from the account described in subsection (a) in connection with a claim alleging a violation of section 1311(a) or 1316(a) of this title by an employing office (other than an employing office of the House of Representatives or an employing office of the Senate), the Executive Director shall notify the head of the employing office that the payment has been made, and shall include in the notification a statement of the amount of the payment.

children.heading: Reimbursement by office
children.label: 2 Reimbursement by office
children.text: Not later than 180 days after receiving a notification from the Executive Director under paragraph (1), the head of the employing office involved shall transfer to the account described in subsection (a), out of any funds available for operating expenses of the office, a payment equal to the amount specified in the notification.

children.heading: Timetable and procedures for reimbursement
children.label: 3 Timetable and procedures for reimbursement
children.text: The head of an employing office shall transfer a payment under paragraph (2) in accordance with such timetable and procedures as may be established under regulations promulgated by the Office.
